questions.question: What is the maximum number of SOQL queries allowed in a synchronous Apex transaction?

choices.label: A
choices.explanation: 50 is incorrect.

choices.label: B
choices.explanation: Synchronous transactions allow 100 SOQL queries.

choices.label: C
choices.explanation: 150 applies to DML rows, not queries.

choices.label: D
choices.explanation: 200 is not a governor limit value here.

questions.explanation: Synchronous Apex allows 100 SOQL queries per transaction.

questions.question: Which keyword on an Apex class enforces the running user's record sharing rules?

choices.label: A
choices.explanation: without sharing bypasses sharing.

choices.label: B
choices.explanation: Inherited sharing defers to the caller.

choices.label: C
choices.explanation: with sharing enforces the user's sharing rules.

choices.label: D
choices.explanation: private is an access modifier, unrelated to sharing.

questions.explanation: Use `with sharing` to honor record sharing rules of the running user.

citations.title: Using the with sharing or without sharing Keywords

questions.question: Which field type allows you to enforce uniqueness across all records in a custom object?

choices.label: A
choices.explanation: Plain Text isn't unique by default.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Text with the Unique attribute checked
choices.explanation: Marking the field Unique enforces no-duplicates.

choices.label: C
choices.explanation: Auto Number is always unique but doesn't accept arbitrary values.

choices.label: D
choices.explanation: Picklists are not unique fields.

questions.explanation: Marking a text/email/number field as Unique enforces uniqueness.

questions.question: What is the minimum overall Apex code coverage required to deploy to production?

choices.label: A
choices.explanation: 65% is below the production minimum.

choices.label: B
choices.explanation: Production deploys require 75% overall Apex coverage.

choices.label: C
choices.explanation: 85% is a common internal target but not the platform minimum.

choices.label: D
choices.explanation: 100% is not required by the platform.

questions.explanation: Salesforce requires 75% overall Apex code coverage to deploy to a production org.

questions.question: Which search language searches across multiple objects and field types in a single query?

choices.label: A
choices.explanation: SOQL queries one object at a time (with subqueries for related).

choices.label: B
choices.explanation: SOSL searches across many objects at once.

choices.label: C
choices.explanation: SQL is not a Salesforce query language.

choices.label: D
choices.explanation: GraphQL is supported but isn't the cross-object search language here.

questions.explanation: SOSL (Salesforce Object Search Language) is designed for full-text cross-object search.
